challenges.title: Serve Receive Formation
challenges.description: The opponent just served. Set up your team in the classic 'W' serve receive — 3 back-row passers in a W shape and 2 front-row players ready near the net.
challenges.hint: Your 3 passers form a W in the back court. The setter and one hitter wait near the net.
challenges.success_message: Perfect W formation! Your passers cover the whole back court and your setter is ready to set.
challenges.learn_tip: The W formation puts your best passers — often the Libero in the middle back — where most serves land.

challenges.title: Spiking Attack
challenges.description: Your team has the ball and the setter just set it high on the left side. Arrange your players — blockers jump at the net, hitters approach, and back row covers tips.
challenges.hint: Two blockers at the net defend. The outside hitter approaches from the left. Back row players cover the court.
challenges.success_message: Textbook attack formation! Blockers up, hitter approaching, back row ready to cover the tip.
challenges.learn_tip: When your team attacks, back row players 'cover' the hitter — ready to dig if the block sends it back.

challenges.title: Defensive Block
challenges.description: The other team's outside hitter is about to spike from the left side. Get your team into defensive position — two blockers at the net and back row in defensive spots.
challenges.hint: Two players jump at the net to block. The other four drop back in a defensive base formation.
challenges.success_message: Great defensive setup! Two blockers seal the net and back row is spread to cover the whole court.
challenges.learn_tip: The libero almost always plays center back — they're your best passer and get the most digs from that spot.
